overview.heading: Topics
overview.subtitle: In an age of information overload, truth and rumor blur together — the same event can spark a dozen conflicting accounts. We gather reporting scattered across the globe, focus it into a coherent timeline, summarize what's known, and rate the credibility of every source we reference.
overview.empty: No topics match the current filters

intro.pipelineBody: Every new report is checked against what's already being tracked: it might continue a thread we're already following (a new event, or just corroboration of one already reported — no extra AI research spent), or it might be significant enough to start a thread of its own; anything less certain goes into a watchlist first. For each new event, AI cross-checks across sources (regardless of language), searches public information, and produces a summary and timeline, while recording each source and rating its credibility.
intro.pipelineImageAlt: A topic card showing its title, credibility tag, domain/region tags, and a world map
intro.pipelineCaption: A topic card: title, credibility tag, regions, and stats at a glance
intro.charterBody: Every actively-tracked topic carries a compact "charter": its current state in one line, why it matters, what should (and shouldn't) count as a continuation of it, and the key actors involved. New reports are matched against these criteria to decide where they belong, and the criteria themselves are refreshed as the story develops. A topic that's gone quiet for a while is marked "dormant," and eventually archived if nothing new comes in — you'll see the matching tag in the corner of its card.
intro.charterImageAlt: A topic page's charter block, showing the current state, why it matters, inclusion criteria, and key actors sections
intro.charterCaption: A topic's charter: current state, why it matters, inclusion criteria, key actors
intro.linkCheckBody: Every external link cited during AI research is checked with a real request before it's saved. Dead links are swapped for the page actually visited during research — or dropped entirely if no reliable substitute exists — so you never land on a broken page.
intro.linkCheckCaption: Every external link is actually opened and checked before it's saved
intro.credibilityIntro: Credibility ratings apply to an individual report or an individual claim — not to an overall judgment of a media outlet or journalist. Ratings weigh whether a source is official or authoritative, whether it's attributed by name, and whether it's corroborated by other independent sources.
intro.credibilityImageAlt: A source card showing the source name, credibility tag, and the reasoning behind its rating
intro.credibilityCaption: Every source's rating comes with a concrete reason
intro.topicTiersImageAlt: Close-up of the credibility tag in the corner of a topic card
intro.topicTiersCaption: The tag in a topic card's corner reflects where verification currently stands

topicTier.VERIFIED: Cross-checked across multiple sources with consistent, well-supported reporting.
topicTier.DEVELOPING: Still being actively tracked — information may be incomplete or still changing.
topicTier.DISPUTED: Sources give noticeably inconsistent or conflicting accounts of the same event.
topicTier.UNVERIFIED: Currently based on only one or a few sources, not yet cross-checked.

intro.sourceTiersImageAlt: A source card showing its credibility tier tag
intro.sourceTiersCaption: Individual sources ranked by credibility tier

sourceTier.OFFICIAL: Published directly by an official body, involved party, or first-hand authoritative source.
sourceTier.HIGH: Named reporting from a named outlet, corroborated by other sources.
sourceTier.MEDIUM: Has a named source, but not yet corroborated by enough other sources.
sourceTier.LOW: Source isn't clearly named, or its account diverges from other sources.
sourceTier.UNVERIFIED: A source or claim still awaiting further verification.

intro.candidatesBody: Some signals aren't yet substantial enough to become their own topic, but aren't irrelevant either — these go into a watchlist first, and we keep an eye on whether more days and more sources corroborate them. Once there's enough evidence, a signal gets promoted to a fully tracked topic; you can always see why something hasn't made the cut yet.
intro.candidatesImageAlt: A watchlist card showing its title, why it's being watched, and how many times it's been mentioned
intro.candidatesCaption: A candidate: title, why it's being watched, and how many times it's come up

topic.searchEmpty: No events match the current search
topic.criteriaHeading: What counts as part of this thread

candidates.intro: The watchlist tracks signals that haven't yet accumulated enough evidence to become a fully tracked thread. The system keeps an eye on them and promotes a signal to a thread once it's been corroborated across multiple days and sources — so you can always see why something hasn't made the cut yet.
candidates.empty: The watchlist is empty right now

event.researchingHint: This event is still being cross-checked across sources. Check back soon for the full summary and ranked sources.

log.heading: Log
log.pageIntro: 逆風 continuously monitors a range of public news and financial-information platforms, periodically compiling their publicly published headlines and summaries through an automated process, as reference material for later cross-source comparison, fact-checking, and credibility assessment. Below is the daily activity log — click into a day to see its reference materials and extended discussion in detail.
log.summaryLine: Compiled {{articleCount}} reference items ‧ researched {{researchCount}} events in depth

terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to 逆風 ("the Service"). These Terms of Service describe the nature of the Service, its content sources, and rules for use. By using the Service, you agree to the terms below. This page is a general statement, not individualized legal advice — please consult qualified counsel for specific legal disputes.
terms.natureBody: The Service is a news-event organization and credibility-assessment tool that automatically tracks multiple public news and financial-information platforms, arranges reports describing the same event into a timeline, and provides credibility assessments based on a public methodology. The Service is not itself a news publisher and does not publish original news content; all timeline summaries and credibility scores are generated by the system according to its stated methodology, may contain errors or delays, are provided for reference only, and constitute no warranty of any kind.
terms.copyrightBody: For each original report, the Service provides only the headline, a summary written by the Service itself, and a hyperlink to the original article — it does not reproduce or repost the full original text. This approach relies on Taiwan's Copyright Act Article 52 (quotation of already-published works, within reasonable limits, for reporting, commentary, or other legitimate purposes) and Article 61 (public transmission online of commentary on current political, economic, or social affairs, unless the copyright holder has stated that reproduction or transmission is not permitted). If a copyright holder does not wish their commentary on current affairs to be compiled and presented by the Service, they may state that reproduction is not permitted under the proviso of Article 61, or contact the Service as described in Section 9 below so the matter can be addressed. A plain hyperlink to a third-party site is not considered reproduction or distribution of that site's content.
terms.credibilityBody: Credibility ratings and tiers on the Service are assessments of the verification results for an individual report or an individual claim, based on a public methodology considering factors such as how official or named the source is and cross-source corroboration. These ratings do not represent, and do not constitute, a negative assessment of the overall character, reputation, or goodwill of any media organization, journalist, or individual. Ratings may change as verification progresses or the methodology is updated.
terms.disclaimerBody: The Service is provided "as is," with no express or implied warranty as to the accuracy, timeliness, or completeness of its content. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Service is not liable for any direct or indirect loss arising from use of, or inability to use, the Service. Event timelines and source rankings are generated automatically or semi-automatically from public information and may diverge from fact due to source errors, AI misjudgment, or delayed updates; users should independently verify content before citing or repeating it.
terms.privacyBody: The Service currently offers no account registration or login, and does not actively collect personal data such as names or contact details. Interface preferences (e.g., language, light/dark mode) and browsing history are stored only on the user's own device (localStorage), are never transmitted to a server, and are not used to identify any individual. If the Service later adds features involving the collection, processing, or use of personal data, a privacy policy will be published separately in accordance with the then-current Personal Data Protection Act (and its subsequent amendments and implementing regulations), including data-security and, where applicable, breach-notification obligations.
terms.conductBody: Users must use the Service lawfully and must not: bulk-scrape the Service's content by automated means for separate commercial use, circumvent or disrupt the Service's technical protection measures, or use the Service's content as a basis for defaming or harassing others. The Service reserves the right to restrict or terminate access where abuse is identified.
terms.changesBody: The Service may revise these terms at any time due to regulatory changes, feature updates, or other reasonable cause; revised terms will be posted on this page with an updated "last updated" date above. Continued use of the Service after a revision constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
terms.governingLawBody: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of China (Taiwan). Any dispute arising from the Service shall be subject to the Taipei District Court as the court of first instance, except where mandatory law provides otherwise.
terms.contactBody: The Service is currently operated by an individual as a utility tool and does not yet offer a formal support mailbox or online appeal form. For questions about how content sources are handled or about these terms, please watch for contact channels the Service may announce in the future.
